rationale: Best existing central royal-site signal: Humboldt Forum/old palace architecture near Museum Island, strong enough for a cover/card crop.
alt: Humboldt Forum palace-style architecture in central Berlin, the main starting point for reading Hohenzollern Berlin on foot
caption: Caption: Start Hohenzollern Berlin at the palace site, now the Humboldt Forum, before reading the cathedral and Museum Island around it.

rationale: Direct post-renovation 2026 crypt image, the most specific factual image for the article.
alt: Hohenzollern Crypt inside the Berliner Dom after its 2026 reopening
caption: Caption: The Hohenzollern Crypt is the most direct royal-family layer below the Berliner Dom.

sourceType: reused approved BerlinWalk image pack / Wikimedia Commons
rationale: Clean blue-hour Bebelplatz/Staatsoper view for the Unter den Linden royal-stage section.
alt: Bebelplatz and the Staatsoper near Unter den Linden, part of Berlin’s ceremonial royal boulevard
caption: Caption: Unter den Linden works best as a sequence of royal, cultural and state spaces, not just a street transfer.

sourceType: reused approved BerlinWalk image pack / Wikimedia Commons
rationale: Strong Museum Island visual with warm light, useful for the Prussian cultural-landscape section.
alt: Altes Museum on Berlin’s Museum Island at sunset, showing the cultural landscape beside royal Berlin
caption: Caption: Museum Island makes more sense when you see it beside the palace site and cathedral.

sourceType: reused approved BerlinWalk image pack / Wikimedia Commons
rationale: Strong palace exterior with water depth; clear residence signal for the Charlottenburg add-on.
alt: Charlottenburg Palace and reflecting water in Berlin, the clearest royal residence add-on for Hohenzollern history
caption: Caption: Charlottenburg is the add-on when you want royal Berlin to feel like a residence, not only a city-centre memory layer.
